title: Investigate service install failure on low-disk host
description:
On host alias ub, `rakkib pull` stopped during step 5 while deploying `hermes-agent`. Reproduction on the host with `docker compose up -d` in `/srv/docker/hermes-agent` fails with: `failed to extract layer ... no space left on device` under `/var/lib/containerd/.../overlayfs`. The host only has a 19G root volume and showed 7.8G free before reproduction, then 4.0G free after retry. The `nousresearch/hermes-agent:latest` image includes a 1.847 GB compressed layer and needs more extraction space than the host has available. Also observed: `rakkib status` listed many services as installed even though only six containers were actually running and later service directories were empty, so status is overstating successful deployment after a mid-wave failure.

Acceptance:
- Investigate and document minimum disk expectations or preflight checks for large service images like hermes-agent
- `rakkib pull` should fail with a clearer disk-capacity message before or at service startup
- `rakkib status` should not report services as installed when they were only selected/rendered but never started successfully
notes: Implemented service resource guardrails for heavy installs. Added registry-driven resource_requirements for hermes-agent, selection UI warning suffixes in both interview and add flows, and per-service preflight enforcement before Docker pull/start with clear filesystem-backed disk messaging. Preflight now hard-fails below min_* and warns below recommended_*; disk errors explicitly explain that Docker storage free space on the backing filesystem matters more than raw VM disk size.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create slim runtime branch for curl installer
description: The current curl-pipe installer downloads install.sh from main, then install.sh clones the full main branch. That brings dev-only project files onto target servers, including .beads, .claude, .opencode, docs, services planning docs, tests, and web assets. Create a dedicated runtime branch that contains only the files Rakkib needs to run, and update the installer flow so the existing public command still works: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/FayaaDev/Rakkib/main/install.sh | bash. Proposed approach: create a runtime branch; keep install.sh, pyproject.toml, README.md if useful, .gitignore, and src/rakkib/** including src/rakkib/data/**; remove dev/project files from that branch such as .beads/, .claude/, .opencode/, docs/, services/, tests/, web/, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, WebUI.md, and pyqr.md. Update install.sh on main so BRANCH defaults to runtime instead of main. Update the clone command to use a shallow single-branch clone: git clone --depth 1 --single-branch --branch "$BRANCH" "$REPO_URL" "$INSTALL_DIR". Apply the same install.sh default on the runtime branch so future updates track runtime. Runtime code currently appears package-data driven under src/rakkib/data via pyproject.toml package-data, and rakkib.steps.services resolves templates from Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / data, so src/rakkib/data must stay in the runtime branch. Acceptance criteria: the existing curl command still fetches install.sh from main; default install clones runtime; users can still override with RAKKIB_BRANCH or --branch; git ls-tree -r runtime --name-only shows only runtime-required files; dev-only directories are absent from runtime; bare-metal validation runs bash install.sh in a clean checkout or the curl-pipe command on Ubuntu 24.04 per project rules.
close_reason: Closed

5. Post-install add: rakkib add jellyfin → runs only Jellyfin slice.

notes:
Investigation update (2026-05-14)
- `rakkib web --lan` code path appears correct: it binds `0.0.0.0`, prints a LAN URL based on `detect_lan_ip()`, and token bootstrap is only relevant after the browser already reaches the app.
- The reported failure now includes both with-token and without-token URLs failing on the phone, which shifts the likely cause away from token/bootstrap logic and toward plain network reachability.
- Current host observations from investigation:
  - route-based LAN IP detection would advertise `192.168.0.235`
  - host is on normal `192.168.0.0/24` via `enp1s0`
  - not a VM in this environment (`systemd-detect-virt` => none)
  - nothing was listening on `:8080` at the moment of inspection, so a live listener check during an active `rakkib web --lan` session is still needed
  - `ufw` is not installed; nftables/firewall state was not confirmed because non-root inspection was permission-limited
- Most likely buckets now:
  1. `rakkib web --lan` was not actually listening when tested
  2. host firewall/nftables blocked inbound `8080`
  3. router/client isolation blocked phone-to-host traffic despite same SSID
- Recommended next evidence to capture later:
  1. `ss -ltnp '( sport = :8080 )'` while `rakkib web --lan --no-open` is running
  2. `curl -i http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/health`
  3. `curl -i http://192.168.0.235:8080/api/health`
  4. exact phone browser error text when opening `http://192.168.0.235:8080/api/health`
